prompt: I need a LinkedIn DM to Sarah Chen, VP of Revenue Operations at Datawise Analytics (Series B, 180 employees, sells a BI platform to mid-market finance teams). She posted on LinkedIn two days ago about her team cutting their Salesforce-to-NetSuite sync errors by 70% after rebuilding their integration layer in-house. My company is RevStack -- we sell a rev-ops automation platform. I'm Jamie Torres, Senior AE. The hook is her LinkedIn post about the integration rebuild. My ask should be a 15-minute call. Keep it tight for LinkedIn -- under 100 words.
expectations: Message opens with a direct reference to Sarah Chen's LinkedIn post about cutting Salesforce-to-NetSuite sync errors by 70% (drives reply rate) — the first sentence must name her specific achievement, not RevStack or Jamie, because prospect-first openers outperform sender-first openers in cold outreach response rates

Message body is 100 words or fewer and the single CTA is a 15-minute call with no alternative asks or vague 'let me know' language (drives deal velocity) — a single specific ask with a defined time commitment converts at higher rates than multi-option or open-ended closes

Message avoids corporate jargon (leverage, synergy, best-in-class, seamless, robust, solution, game-changing, revolutionary, disruptive, holistic, end-to-end, cutting-edge, world-class, innovative, empower, unlock, transform) — messages with these words have measurably lower reply rates in B2B cold outreach

Output includes a Five Laws compliance audit section verifying all 5 laws (specific reference, lead with them, one ask, word count, banned language) — this structured self-check reduces rep review time and catches messages that would get ignored before they're sent

Output includes a Day 3 follow-up on a different angle (not 'just checking in') and via a different channel since primary was LinkedIn, plus a Day 7 follow-up in single-question format (drives deal velocity) — a pre-built multi-touch sequence saves the rep 15-20 minutes of drafting and keeps the cadence moving without manual effort

Notes section includes the specific hook used, tone classification, and a confidence rating tied to verifiable signal quality (drives pipeline hygiene) — this metadata lets a sales manager audit outreach quality at scale and ensures reps only send messages backed by real prospect signals
